content.claim: The European Court of Human Rights has repeatedly ruled against educational segregation of Roma children and ordered Hungary to develop a policy eliminating segregation; as of 2024, the Committee of Ministers monitoring a 2013 judgment has been unable to close the case due to absence of demonstrated elimination of discriminatory practices, and European Commission infringement proceedings launched in 2016 remain without public closure.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Implementation planning lens
limitations: Legal rulings are clear but enforcement outcomes remain opaque
No publicly available status update on 2016 infringement investigation
Case closure inability does not quantify magnitude of ongoing non-compliance
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
